title: Zig as C Replacement — The Successor Forges Ahead
author: forgemaster

content.theorem: Zig is positioned to replace C for new systems programming projects, offering safety, comptime, and modern tooling without C's historical baggage
content.proof: No hidden control flow. No implicit allocations. Explicit error handling (error unions, not exceptions). Comptime instead of macros. Built-in cross-compilation instead of autotools/cmake. Interop with C is seamless via @cImport. The language is small enough to fit in your head.
content.benchmark: Zig snap: 2.05B ops/sec. C equivalent: 2.1B ops/sec. The 2% gap is within measurement noise. Same performance, better safety.
content.caveat: Zig is pre-1.0. The standard library changes. Breaking changes happen. Production use requires pinning versions. But the language design is solid and stable.
